questions.q: Motilal Nehru was the father of which future Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Motilal Nehru was the father of Jawaharlal Nehru, independent India's first Prime Minister.

questions.q: What was Motilal Nehru's profession before he entered active politics?
questions.explanation: Motilal Nehru was a leading and highly successful lawyer.

questions.q: The 1928 report on India's constitutional future that Motilal Nehru chaired is commonly called the what?
questions.explanation: The committee he chaired produced the Nehru Report of 1928.

questions.q: Motilal Nehru co-founded which party within the Congress in the early 1920s?
questions.explanation: He co-founded the Swaraj Party to contest legislative council elections.

questions.q: Which high office in the Indian National Congress did Motilal Nehru hold more than once?
questions.explanation: Motilal Nehru served as president of the Indian National Congress.

questions.q: Motilal Nehru's family home in Allahabad, a centre of nationalist activity, was named what?
questions.explanation: The Nehru family residence in Allahabad was known as Anand Bhawan.

questions.q: The Nehru family belonged to which community of Brahmins originally from the Kashmir region?
questions.explanation: The Nehrus were Kashmiri Pandits whose ancestors had migrated from Kashmir.

questions.q: Motilal Nehru practised law primarily in which city of the United Provinces?
questions.explanation: He built his legal career mainly at the Allahabad High Court.

questions.q: The Swaraj Party that Motilal Nehru helped found aimed to do what?
questions.explanation: The Swaraj Party sought to enter the legislative councils to obstruct colonial rule from within.

questions.q: In which decade did Motilal Nehru die?
questions.explanation: Motilal Nehru died in 1931, in the 1930s.

questions.q: In which year was Motilal Nehru born?
questions.explanation: Motilal Nehru was born on 6 May 1861 in Agra.

questions.q: Along with Motilal Nehru, who was the other principal co-founder of the Swaraj Party?
questions.explanation: Motilal Nehru founded the Swaraj Party in 1923 together with Chittaranjan (C. R.) Das.

questions.q: The Nehru Report of 1928 was drafted as a response to a challenge thrown by which British official?
questions.explanation: Secretary of State Lord Birkenhead challenged Indians to produce an agreed constitution, prompting the Nehru Report.

questions.q: Which constitutional status did the Nehru Report of 1928 demand for India?
options: A federation under the Crown forever
questions.explanation: The Nehru Report recommended dominion status for India, a point that disappointed younger radicals.

questions.q: Motilal Nehru first presided over the Indian National Congress at which session?
questions.explanation: Motilal Nehru first served as Congress president at the Amritsar session in 1919.

questions.q: Motilal Nehru served a second term as Congress president at which session?
questions.explanation: He presided over the Congress a second time at the Calcutta session of 1928.

questions.q: Motilal Nehru founded which prominent English-language nationalist newspaper in 1919?
questions.explanation: He founded the newspaper 'The Independent' in Allahabad in 1919.

questions.q: A controversial demand within the Nehru Report concerning Muslim representation led to opposition from which leader?
questions.explanation: Jinnah opposed the Nehru Report and responded with his Fourteen Points on Muslim representation.

questions.q: Under the influence of Gandhi's movement, Motilal Nehru famously gave up which symbol of his earlier prosperous lifestyle?
options: His Western luxuries and foreign clothes
questions.explanation: Drawn to the Non-Cooperation Movement, Motilal Nehru abandoned his lavish Western lifestyle and foreign clothes for khadi.

questions.q: The all-parties committee that produced the Nehru Report was convened largely to counter which all-British body?
questions.explanation: The Nehru Report was the Indian answer to the all-British Simon Commission's exclusion of Indians.

questions.q: Motilal Nehru's daughter, sister of Jawaharlal and herself a freedom fighter and later diplomat, was named what?
questions.explanation: His daughter Vijaya Lakshmi Pandit became a noted nationalist and later India's diplomat and UN General Assembly president.

questions.q: Motilal Nehru's wife, mother of Jawaharlal, was named what?
questions.explanation: Motilal Nehru's wife was Swaruprani Thussu, the mother of Jawaharlal and his sisters.

questions.q: On what date in 1931 did Motilal Nehru die?
questions.explanation: Motilal Nehru died on 6 February 1931 in Lucknow.

questions.q: Besides Motilal Nehru as chairman, his son Jawaharlal served as secretary of the committee that drafted the Nehru Report. Which leader's son, Subhas Chandra Bose aside, also pressed for the rival 'independence' resolution at this time?
options: Jawaharlal Nehru himself led the younger radicals
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru, with Subhas Chandra Bose, led younger members who wanted complete independence rather than the dominion status the report endorsed.

questions.q: In which city was Motilal Nehru born in 1861, shortly after his father's death?
questions.explanation: Motilal Nehru was born in Agra in 1861; his father Gangadhar had died shortly before his birth.

questions.q: The Swaraj Party founded by Motilal Nehru and C. R. Das emerged after disagreements within Congress following which event?
options: The suspension of the Non-Cooperation Movement
questions.explanation: The Swaraj Party arose in 1922-23 over whether to enter councils after Non-Cooperation was suspended post-Chauri Chaura.

questions.q: The Nehru Report included a proposed bill of rights. Which of these was among the fundamental rights it advocated?
options: Freedom of conscience and equality before law
questions.explanation: The Nehru Report proposed a set of fundamental rights including freedom of conscience and equality before the law.

questions.q: In the Central Legislative Assembly, the Swaraj Party under Motilal Nehru's leadership functioned chiefly as what?
options: The principal opposition obstructing government measures
questions.explanation: The Swarajists entered the Assembly to act as an obstructionist opposition, repeatedly defeating or embarrassing the government.

questions.q: Motilal Nehru briefly served as Leader of which body, leading the Swaraj Party's parliamentary work in the mid-1920s?
options: The Central Legislative Assembly Swarajist group
questions.explanation: He led the Swaraj Party group in the Central Legislative Assembly, becoming a leading voice of the opposition.

questions.q: Motilal Nehru donated the family's grand original residence to the nation; what new name and use did the Nehrus adopt for Anand Bhawan after gifting the old house?
options: It was sold to the British government
The old house became Swaraj Bhawan, gifted to Congress
It became a hospital named after Kamala
questions.explanation: The original Anand Bhawan was donated to the Congress and renamed Swaraj Bhawan, while a new Anand Bhawan was built nearby.
